desc: Round-5 C4-emit: a `ref="save"` whose name equals an `$emit('save')` event. On Angular an emit mints a `save = output()` class member AND the ref minted a `save = viewChild()` member → duplicate member (TS2300, silent — no scope-walk/nested-this signal). R4-A's deconflictRefsAgainstUserBindings collision surface covered state/computed/prop/user-binding names but not emit-output names. Fix (angular emitAngular.ts): include `ir.emits.map(sanitizeEventName)` in the userBindingNames passed to the pass, so the INTERNAL ref renames to `saveRef` (field + viewChild selector + #template-ref); the `save` output stays. Angular-only — React/Svelte emits lower to `onX` prop callbacks (no `save` member), so their callers are unchanged and byte-identical. Found by the shape-matrix harness angular-dup-member invariant (ref-rename-cross/ref-name-equals-emit).
